TextENGNew: The rain was still falling softly, without pause.
changeReason: Original incorrectly translates しとしと (gentle, drizzling rain) as 'pouring down'; new translation accurately conveys the soft, steady rainfall.

TextENGNew: The minivan that should have been at the bottom of the stone steps... was definitely gone.
I stood in the spot where the minivan should have been... and my mind drifted back to that moment.
After seeing the car with the suicide victims, Miyuki-chan and I had come here...
changeReason: Original adds 'right after' and uses 'discovering' while JPN 見た後 simply means 'after seeing'; new translation is more accurate.

TextENGNew: When I peered inside, I'd noticed something swaying from the driver's seat.
Something...?
changeReason: JPN 何か…？ is a one-word interjection 'Something...?' not a full question 'What was it?'; new translation matches the original's fragmentary quality.

TextENGNew: That's right. It was... a keychain. A keychain had been dangling from the steering column.
changeReason: Old says 'from the keyhole' but ハンドルのところ means near the steering wheel.

TextENGNew: Which would mean... wait...
That meant the keys had been left in the ignition the whole time...?!
changeReason: JPN キーが差しっぱなし specifically means keys were left 'inserted' (in the ignition); new translation adds this precision vs the vague 'left inside the car'.

TextENGNew: Incredibly careless, but perhaps Towada-san had figured it was safe enough in an abandoned village with no one around...?
changeReason: JPN 無用心極まりない means 'extremely/incredibly careless'; original softens this to 'might seem careless' while new translation preserves the emphatic judgment.

TextENGNew: But... because the keys were left in, someone had stolen the car...
changeReason: Original uses hypothetical 'would've been able to steal'; new translation uses factual past 'someone had stolen', matching JPN past tense 盗まれてしまった (was stolen).

TextENGNew: (Stolen...?)
I fell into thought. ...Come to think of it, the car Miyuki-chan and I had arrived in also had its keys left in.
changeReason: Original incorrectly says 'Miyuki-chan and I left the keys to her car' implying ownership; JPN 乗ってきた車 means 'the car we came in' without specifying ownership.

TextENGNew: If the car was stolen, that meant there was a thief.
changeReason: Original misinterprets as a hypothetical about their own car being stolen; JPN states a logical deduction: 'If it was stolen, there must be a thief' — a key reasoning step about someone else being present.

TextENGNew: So, in other words... this had nothing to do with occult phenomena like curses or being demoned away.
There's still a possibility... that there are more people in this village than just us...
Then who the hell could it be...? And for what...?
Or... was this a trap meant to lure us all somewhere...?
Or maybe... this really is the work of demons?
Could it be the work of the dead meddling in the lives of the living? Cutting off our means of escape and then strangling us to death...?
My thoughts were going around in circles.
Just who was it that was "alive" in this village right now...?
I hit the limit of my reasoning. At this point, my thoughts had become incoherent.
Right. My thoughts went astray when I questioned if it was the work of the living or the dead.
There's no way it could be anything besides another living person... It can't be.
Just then... I heard the sound of a person approaching from behind.
*scrape*
I heard a footstep, and at the same time I suddenly felt someone place a hand on my back.
"Uwah?!"
I arched my back backwards while turning around.
Otobe was standing there with his mouth hanging open.
"A-Arakawa-san...?"
"What? Otobe?!@k@vS10/62/410100710. Don't surprise me like that!"
"Sorry, I didn't mean to surprise you...@k@vS10/41/410200230. But Arakawa-san, you came to a sudden stop and you looked upset, so I wanted to ask if something was wrong."
"Oh, no. It's nothing.@k@vS10/62/410100712. Just a little lost in thought.@k@vS10/62/410100713. ...Let's hurry up and get going!"
"But... which way should we go...?"
An unpaved gravel road extended to both the left and the right.
"Left or right...?"
"I'm fine with either one..."
Otobe started walking to the left.
"No... let's go this way."
I reflexively grabbed Otobe's arm and started walking to the right.
Why did I want to go this direction? Even I wasn't sure, myself.
But Miyuki-chan and I originally came from the direction to the right, so maybe I just felt some desire to go back the way I came from.
"You want to go... this way...?"
Otobe timidly followed behind me.
No matter how far we went, I couldn't see anything but trees. It was just one long road extending straight through a dense forest.

"Arakawa-san...@k@vS10/41/410200235. Mion-san... went out searching a while ago, didn't she?@k@vS10/41/410200236. ...If a local like Mion-san couldn't find anything... do you really think it's possible for us to find anything out here...?"
"I dunno, but do we really have any other choice?@k@vS10/62/410100717. Kurosawa suddenly disappeared on us."
"That's true, but..."
"But what...?"
But Otobe swallowed his words and awkwardly looked down.
...I had a good idea what he was trying to say.
If Kurosawa was demoned away, there was nothing we could do about it. Anyone caught by the demons would simply be devoured and thrown away, never to be found again.
Otobe's eyes seemed to say as much, and somewhere in my heart I was thinking the same thing.
"It's nothing..."
"Then don't say anything..."
After that, the two of us said nothing more.
We simply walked down the muddy road.
A dirt trail extended between the trees, seeming to invite us in.
"Should we head this way...?"
"Don't be stupid!@k@vS10/62/410100721. If we head down a pitch-black trail like that, we'll never make it out, ok?"
"...Yeah."
My flashlight could only illuminate the ground two or three meters from my feet.
No matter how far we go, we're as likely to find a car or a person as we are to find a tanuki.
"I guess... it's useless..."
"Useless...?"
"Because even if we go further, we won't find anything...@k@vS10/41/410200243. If someone dragged him into those trees, we'll never find him!"
"...And who dragged him, then?!"
"Well..."
Otobe very quietly whispered the word "demons," but I pretended not to hear him.
In fact, it could've just been my ears playing tricks on me. Maybe he said something else.
Should we head back to the assembly hall and wait for morning...?
No, then it'll be too late.
The man-eating demons might kill all of us in the middle of the night.
...I felt sick. Unpleasant thoughts spun round and round inside my head.
It had become difficult to distinguish delusions from reality.
There really might be demons out there.
Or, maybe not.
"Arakawa-san... it's impossible to keep going...@k@vS10/41/410200246. Let's head back."
"We can't turn back like this!"
"Why not?!@k@vS10/41/410200248. What's wrong with heading back?!@k@vS10/41/410200249. ...I've had enough of this."
Otobe finally stopped.
"Otobe..."
Tch...
I quietly clicked my tongue to myself. ...What a pathetic human being.
If you value your life, then show some guts, damn it.
"Alright... you want to go back?"
"But... do we really have no choice but to go back...?"
Otobe was acting confused and indecisive.
And, so..!
I started getting irritated and glared at Otobe. As soon as I did, he started to look upset.
"But... even if we keep going on, I don't think we can do anything about it."
"I'm starting to feel the same way...!@k@vS10/62/410100728. ...Well, what should we do?"
"So... I think, umm..."
Though I had an umbrella in hand, my pants were still soaked with mud and rain. Plus, my hands and feet were freezing.
The two of us suddenly spoke up at the same time.
"Let's keep going."@r@t"Let's turn back."
I had to sigh at our completely contradictory decisions. ...It's tough working with someone of a completely different disposition... Clearly, we made a terrible team.
Still... I didn't have the stomach to handle this situation on my own. Otobe probably felt the same way.
So, my decisions had just as much weight on my partner as they did on me...
"Even if we go any further, it's just a waste..."

"But that doesn't mean we can just go back to the assembly hall and quietly wait to die, right?@k@vS10/62/410100731. Even if our phones can't connect, we can still walk to the police.@k@vS10/62/410100732. Okinomiya's not too far from here."
"Not too far...?@k@vS10/41/410200256. But... we're traveling by foot, aren't we?!"
Sure enough, I also thought it sounded reckless.
But... what choice did we have?!
"Oh... that's right!"
That moment, I felt inspiration like a divine revelation.
"We can drive there!"
"In what car...?"
Otobe began to furrow his eyebrows, but I continued.
"I think we should return to the assembly hall."
"Huh...?@k@vS10/40/410300504. You're not going to search for Kurosawa-san?"
Miyuki-chan furrowed her eyebrows and stared at me as soon as she heard my answer.
Somehow, it felt like the tone of her voice was criticizing me.
"Well, I do want to search for him.@k@vS10/62/410100738. But it's already pitch black outside.@k@vS10/62/410100739. If we go out searching right now, there's a risk even more danger will befall us."
"Plus, it's still raining...@k@vS10/62/410100741. Wouldn't it be easier to look for him after the sun comes up?"
The look on Miyuki-chan's face indicated she still wasn't fully satisfied, but eventually she relented and nodded to me.
"...It's unfortunate, but it's just as Ryuunosuke-san says.@k@vS10/40/410300507. It can't be helped."
"...I'm sorry."
Towada-san walked up next to me and lightly bowed her head to Miyuki-chan.
"...Why are you apologizing, Towada-san?"
"Takumi-kun... caused trouble for you...@k@vS10/42/410600151. That's all..."
"That's just the situation we're in. It can't be helped.@k@vS10/62/410100743. ...Don't worry about it!"
"Okay..."
When I saw Towada-san's shoulders droop... the guilt started to eat at me.
Even though I did believe it couldn't be helped, I still couldn't bring myself to look her eye to eye.
...The rain began coming down even harder.
We hastened our pace to nearly a run, and eventually arrived at the assembly hall.
